text.title: Conventional Commits Enforced at the Hook Core Rules
text.summary: 8 testable rules with rationale + source for gov-conventional-commits-enforced. Includes terminal skip-this-methodology rule.

rule.statement: Every repo MUST install a `commit-msg` hook that runs `commitlint --edit $1`. Hook bypass via `--no-verify` is forbidden by policy and audited.
rule.rationale: Conventional Commits 1.0.0 spec — enforcement at the hook is the only point where messages cannot leak to the remote.

rule.statement: Allowed types: `feat`, `fix`, `chore`, `refactor`, `docs`, `test`, `perf`, `build`, `ci`, `style`, `revert`. Other types MUST be rejected.

rationale.source: Conventional Commits spec + release tooling expectations

rule.rationale: Conventional Commits spec + release tooling expectations — type drift breaks downstream release tools.

rule.statement: Subject line MUST be imperative mood, lowercase first character, no trailing period, ≤72 characters.
rule.rationale: git book ch. 5 — imperative mood is the project standard since 2005.

rule.statement: Breaking changes MUST use `!` after type/scope AND a `BREAKING CHANGE:` footer.
rule.rationale: Conventional Commits spec — both signals required for tool parsers.

rule.statement: CI MUST run `commitlint` against the PR title for squash-merged PRs; squash merges otherwise bypass the hook.
rule.rationale: GitHub squash-merge default behaviour — without PR title check, squash subjects diverge.

rule.statement: Releases MUST be driven by `release-please`, `semantic-release`, or `changesets`; mapping: `feat` → minor, `fix`/`perf` → patch, `BREAKING CHANGE:` → major; chore/docs/test/style/build/ci do NOT bump version.
rule.rationale: release-please / semantic-release docs — deterministic mapping prevents human-error in semver.

rule.statement: Agents MUST only edit `## [Unreleased]` section of CHANGELOG.md; released sections are read-only.
rule.rationale: Keep a Changelog convention — released sections are auditable history.

rule.statement: If repo is single-dev scratch or mirror/vendored, return `skipped`.
